trajectory.description: Observation: Starting fresh with empty workspace. Analysis: Need to build a weather forecasting model for WeatherBench. Task is to predict Z500 and T850 72h ahead from 3 consecutive 6-hourly snapshots. Decision: Create a UNet model, training script, and prediction script. Train for 5 epochs to get baseline and measure time.

trajectory.description: Observation: Baseline UNet gives rmse_z500=494.9, overfits after epoch 1. ~18s/epoch. Analysis: Need residual prediction, bigger model, regularization. Decision: Rewrite with residual prediction (predict change from last timestep), larger UNet (base_dim=128), dropout, and train 20 epochs.

trajectory.command: NUM_EPOCHS=20 BASE_DIM=96 DROPOUT=0.05 LR=3e-4 python train.py
trajectory.description: Observation: Channel mismatch in UpBlock - skip4 has base_dim*8 channels but UpBlock(base_dim*8, base_dim*4) expects concat of out_ch*2=base_dim*8, not base_dim*4+base_dim*8=base_dim*12. Analysis: UpBlock concatenates upsampled (out_ch) with skip (which has different channel count). Need to make ConvBlock input = out_ch + skip_ch. Decision: Fix UpBlock to accept skip_channels parameter, or restructure UNet to match channels properly.

trajectory.description: Observation: Eval failed due to torch.load weights_only=True default in PyTorch 2.6. Training best z500_rmse=460.2. Analysis: Need weights_only=False in predict.py. Decision: Fix predict.py, re-save checkpoint with plain float norm_stats, submit eval.

trajectory.command: python train.py
trajectory.description: Observation: rmse_z500=457.79 with 128-dim 3-level UNet. Analysis: Can improve with deeper architecture, better augmentation, longer training. Decision: Add 4th downsampling level, remove lat flip, train 60 epochs with patience 20.

trajectory.command: NUM_EPOCHS=40 BASE_DIM=128 NUM_LEVELS=3 DROPOUT=0.15 LR=3e-4 python train.py
trajectory.description: Observation: 4-level UNet with base_dim=96 got 478.9, worse than 3-level with base_dim=128 (457.79). Severe overfitting. Analysis: Need to go back to 3-level architecture and focus on regularization. Decision: Use base_dim=128, 3 levels, higher dropout (0.2), higher weight decay, and add test-time augmentation (TTA) with longitude shifts.

trajectory.command: DROPOUT=0.2 WEIGHT_DECAY=0.01 NUM_EPOCHS=50 BASE_DIM=128 NUM_LEVELS=3 LR=3e-4 BATCH_SIZE=32 python train.py
trajectory.description: Observation: Best eval=457.79, training consistently overfits by epoch 4-5. Analysis: Need stronger regularization and possibly different approach. Decision: Try wider model (base_dim=160) with much stronger dropout (0.25), higher weight decay (0.01), and more aggressive augmentation. Also add noise augmentation.

trajectory.command: python train_swa.py
trajectory.description: Observation: Best eval=457.79, training overfits by epoch 4-5. Analysis: 14M params too many for 54K samples. Need smaller model or stronger regularization. Decision: Try smaller model (base_dim=64) with SWA, and also try multi-lead-time training as regularization. First submit current checkpoint to see TTA effect.

Decision: Try FNO (Fourier Neural Operator) which is well-suited for weather prediction on periodic grids. Also try ensemble approach. First submit current checkpoint, then build FNO.
